summary: CC-0080 cleanly removes DB credentials from keystone.conf and sources them via the oslo.config OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION override from a derived <name>-db-connection Secret. All seven acceptance criteria are satisfied: ConfigMap holds only the syntactically-valid placeholder mysql+pymysql://placeholder (tests assert absence of raw AND percent-encoded credential fragments), the derived Secret is created with a controller ownerReference and a single "connection" key via manual Get+Create/Update+drift-check, every pod-spec builder (Deployment, bootstrap Job, all five buildDBJob variants, trust-flush CronJob, fernet-rotate CronJob, credential-rotate CronJob) wires OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via a shared buildDBConnectionEnvVar helper, reconcileDBConnectionSecret sits between reconcileSecrets and reconcileConfig, and SecretsReady=False with reason WaitingForDBCredentials short-circuits before any ConfigMap is written when the upstream Secret is missing. Unit, integration, and Chainsaw E2E coverage is strong; go build and the targeted test subset both pass. Two minor findings recorded below.

minor_issues.title: isMissingSecretOrKey relies on substring match of upstream error text
minor_issues.description: isMissingSecretOrKey falls back to strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found in Secret") to detect a missing data key from secrets.GetSecretValue. If that sentinel message is ever reworded in internal/common/secrets, the reconciler will silently stop translating missing-key errors into the clean SecretsReady=False/WaitingForDBCredentials path and instead surface them as hard reconcile errors — triggering exponential backoff churn rather than the designed polling requeue. Prefer a typed sentinel (e.g. var ErrMissingSecretKey = errors.New(...) in internal/common/secrets, checked via errors.Is) so the coupling between the helper and this caller is compiler-enforced.
minor_issues.evidence: return strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found in Secret") at reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go:176. The producer of that string lives in the shared internal/common/secrets package where the wording is currently a plain fmt.Errorf with no typed cause (per the code comment at lines 166-168).
minor_issues.recommendation: Introduce a sentinel error in internal/common/secrets (e.g. ErrKeyNotFound) and have GetSecretValue wrap it; then this helper can use errors.Is(err, secrets.ErrKeyNotFound) || apierrors.IsNotFound(err). Not blocking — current behavior is correct today and exercised by TestReconcileDBConnectionSecret_UpstreamSecretMissing_RequeueAndCondition.

minor_issues.title: E2E sanity check for derived Secret could detect unencoded password leakage
minor_issues.description: Step 4 asserts the derived Secret value starts with mysql+pymysql:// and is not the placeholder, but does not cross-check that the URL carries the actual upstream password bytes (decoded) — only that it is "a pymysql URL". A regression where reconcileDBConnectionSecret wrote a syntactically-valid but credentially-empty URL (e.g. mysql+pymysql://@host/db due to a username/password swap bug) would still pass. The reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go unit tests cover this, but strengthening the E2E to grep for a known substring (user part) would close the gap at the cluster-integration layer.
minor_issues.evidence: case "$CONN" in mysql+pymysql://*) ;; *) echo FAIL; ... — only asserts scheme prefix.
minor_issues.recommendation: After decoding CONN, also assert it contains the username returned by `kubectl get keystone ... -o jsonpath={.metadata.name}` (managed mode) or the username from the upstream Secret (brownfield). Optional — unit test coverage already guards the invariant.

acceptance_criteria_check.criterion: keystone.conf in the ConfigMap contains no DB password in [database] connection
acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: reconcile_config.go:74-94 sets connection to {{DB_CONNECTION}} then InjectSecrets maps it to dbConnectionPlaceholder = "mysql+pymysql://placeholder". TestReconcileConfig_BrownfieldDatabase_PlaceholderInsteadOfPassword, TestReconcileConfig_ManagedDatabase_NoCredentialsInConfigMap, TestReconcileConfig_SpecialCharactersInCredentials_DoNotLeakToConfigMap all assert NotTo(ContainSubstring(...)) for raw and percent-encoded credentials. Chainsaw step 3 grep -qF against the live Secret's password bytes.

acceptance_criteria_check.criterion: Deployment pod spec includes OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef
acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: reconcile_deployment.go:31-54 defines buildDBConnectionEnvVar; reconcile_deployment.go:172 wires it into keystone-api container. TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_DBConnectionEnv asserts ContainElement(buildDBConnectionEnvVar(ks)).

acceptance_criteria_check.criterion: oslo.config picks up env var and overrides the file
acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: Placeholder is a syntactically valid pymysql URL so oslo.config parses keystone.conf cleanly; OS_<GROUP>__<OPTION> semantics are documented in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md. Chainsaw step 2 gates on status.Ready=True which is only reached when Keystone connects to the DB (bootstrap Job + API probe both pass).

acceptance_criteria_check.criterion: Operator maintains a derived Secret with full connection string
acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go creates/updates <name>-db-connection with single key "connection" and owner reference via controllerutil.SetControllerReference. Drift detection at lines 143-149 replaces Data wholesale. TestReconcileDBConnectionSecret_CreatesSecretWithCorrectURL_Brownfield/_Managed and TestReconcileDBConnectionSecret_UpdatesOnPasswordRotation cover create, managed-mode, and rotation paths.

acceptance_criteria_check.criterion: Password-reading logic moved out of reconcileConfig
acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: reconcile_config.go no longer calls secrets.GetSecretValue for the DB password; only resolveDatabaseHost and dbPort helpers (now shared) remain. The URL assembly (url.UserPassword + resolveDatabaseHost + dbPort + ?charset=utf8) lives in reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go:96-105.

acceptance_criteria_check.criterion: Unit tests cover ConfigMap-has-no-password, Deployment env, derived Secret
Admin bootstrap and Fernet/credential handling unchanged
acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: reconcile_bootstrap.go keeps BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD env; reconcile_fernet.go and reconcile_credential.go only append buildDBConnectionEnvVar to existing env slices. Existing TestReconcileBootstrap_JobCreated updated to assert both env entries (len==2) with ordering. Fernet and credential rotation CronJob tests updated to len==4.

mandatory_pattern_compliance.evidence: reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go:107-158 uses explicit r.Get → apierrors.IsNotFound branch → controllerutil.SetControllerReference + r.Create, otherwise r.Update on drift. No CreateOrUpdate helper that would overwrite immutable fields.

Both Not-Found branches in reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go set conditions.SetCondition with ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation and reason WaitingForDBCredentials. No new subConditionTypes entry because SecretsReady is reused, as stated in the comment at keystone_controller.go:101-103.
All new tests use NewGomegaWithT(t) and . "github.com/onsi/gomega" dot-imports.
TestIntegration_KeystonePodReachesDatabaseViaEnvOverride and TestIntegration_RecreateDerivedSecretWhenDeleted follow the same envtest harness pattern used by siblings (integration_test.go).
No new containers introduced; existing restrictedSecurityContext() calls remain unmodified on every builder.

review_pattern_compliance.evidence: TestReconcile_DBConnectionSecretCreatedBeforeConfigMap uses an interceptor to record Create events and asserts derivedIdx < firstConfigMapIdx. reconcileDBConnectionSecret is placed between reconcileSecrets and reconcileConfig in keystone_controller.go:104-106, with an explanatory comment at :95-103.

buildDBConnectionEnvVar is invoked from Deployment, bootstrap Job, buildDBJob (covering all 5 variants — db-sync/expand/migrate/contract/schema-check, asserted by TestBuildDBJobVariants_DBConnectionEnv), trust-flush CronJob, fernet rotation CronJob, credential rotation CronJob. That enumerates every container in the reconciler that mounts keystone.conf and needs DB access.

TestReconcileConfig_SpecialCharactersInCredentials_DoNotLeakToConfigMap explicitly loops NotTo(ContainSubstring) over raw AND percent-encoded forms. TestReconcileConfig_BrownfieldDatabase_PlaceholderInsteadOfPassword additionally asserts exactly-one-placeholder (strings.Count == 1).

reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go:143-158 computes needsUpdate before calling r.Update (len check + key/value equality check). Confirmed stable via the UID-preservation assertion in TestReconcileDBConnectionSecret_UpdatesOnPasswordRotation.

Create path sets ownerReference before Create; Update path only touches Data[connection], leaving metadata/labels intact. The reconciler short-circuits before any side effect when upstream credentials are missing (no partial derived Secret is ever written).
Every pod-spec builder calls buildDBConnectionEnvVar rather than reconstructing the EnvVar literal. The shared helper eliminates string-duplication risk across six files.

dbConnectionEnvVarName and dbConnectionSecretKey are declared as package-level constants and consumed by the helper. The placeholder string dbConnectionPlaceholder is shared between production and test code.

TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_DerivedDBConnectionSecret verifies the existing secretToKeystoneMapper's isOwnedBy branch enqueues the Keystone owner on derived-Secret events — no new mapper branch is introduced; the name pattern is operator-chosen and correctly not referenced by spec.database.secretRef.

docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md gains the "Database credentials and oslo.config env-var overrides" section (+126 lines), including the enumerated pod-spec builder table and the OS_<GROUP>__<OPTION> syntax reference.
Every item in spec.stories[*].criteria maps to a concrete test in the diff — see acceptance_criteria_check above.

targeted_tests.command: cd operators/keystone && go test -count=1 -run '<CC-0080 test set>' ./internal/controller/...

scope_check.in_scope: All changes are scoped to the DB-credentials-via-Secret/oslo.config env-override refactor: reconcile_dbconnection_secret{.go,_test.go}, reconcile_config{.go,_test.go} (placeholder rendering), six pod-spec builders and their tests wiring OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION, integration_test.go (two new cases), keystone_controller{.go,_test.go} (ordering + watch wiring), docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md (new section), and a new chainsaw E2E under tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets/.
